text: Author Correction Abstract In this paper, we reveal the details of the DeepSeek-R1 model, a new release model, which is diverged from OpenAI’s ChatGPT and should be a viable open-source choice for users. Our model, DeepSeek, which is based on a new design of rule-based reinforcement learning (RL) without any preliminary supervised fine-tuning (SFT), which has been shown to be more efficient. In this paper, we study the multi-stage training scheme and the cold-start strategy to utilize the cold-start data in RL to gain the effective results not only in the technical tasks like reasoning and reward modeling but also in improving the AI training process. It is well known that DeepSeek shows the strength in the technical tasks with an open source to show its decision-making process. Conversely, ChatGPT shows the strength in general tasks as well as creative work. Despite the impressive recent advances, there are many areas which need to be tackled in the field of AI such as data quality issues, black box problems, privacy, and job replacement. We foresee many future developments in the AI domain where our DeepSeek model and other alike models would lead to not only better multi-modal tasks and deeper and wider learning but also to more useful AI landscape and utilities.
